description: Key BEAM runtime health indicators. First stop for on-call triage — process/port saturation, memory pressure, scheduler backlog, GC activity. Generic for any Elixir/Erlang project using prometheus_ex.
title: BEAM / Erlang VM - Overview

k8s_pod.description: Pod name filter — select specific pods to scope all panels to one application
k8s_pod.name: k8s_pod

widgets.description: % of process limit in use. >85% is critical — node will crash at 100%.

filter.expression: deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod
deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod

widgets.title: Process Utilization

widgets.description: % of port limit in use. Ports = sockets, files, NIFs.

filter.expression: deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod
deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod

widgets.title: Port Utilization

widgets.description: % of atom table used. Atom table never shrinks — runaway growth = leak.

filter.expression: deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod
deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod

widgets.title: Atom Utilization

widgets.description: Number of ETS tables currently open.
widgets.title: ETS Tables

widgets.description: CPU scheduler threads available to the BEAM.
widgets.title: Schedulers Online

widgets.description: Stacked composition of BEAM memory — watch for any segment growing unbounded.
widgets.title: Memory Breakdown

widgets.description: Steady monotonic growth indicates a binary leak (unreferenced binaries not GC'd).
widgets.title: Binary Memory (off-heap)

widgets.description: Normal scheduler run queue. Sustained > 0 = CPU saturation.
widgets.title: Run Queue Length

widgets.description: Dirty CPU = long-running NIF pressure. Dirty IO = file/socket I/O pressure.
widgets.title: Dirty CPU / IO Run Queues

widgets.description: Total Erlang processes. Runaway growth = process leak (missing termination or gen_server loop).
widgets.title: Live Processes

widgets.description: Garbage collections per second. Spikes indicate sudden allocation pressure.
widgets.title: GC Rate

widgets.description: Bytes reclaimed per second by the garbage collector.
widgets.title: GC Bytes Reclaimed

widgets.description: Scheduler context switches per second. High rate = many short-lived processes.
widgets.title: Context Switch Rate

widgets.description: BEAM reductions per second — proxy for total CPU work across all processes.
widgets.title: Reductions Rate

widgets.description: >85% is critical — BEAM refuses to spawn new processes at the limit.

filter.expression: deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod
deployment.environment in $environment AND k8s.cluster.name in $k8s_cluster AND k8s.pod.name in $k8s_pod

widgets.title: Process Utilization %
